title: Sri Sri Kalika Maharani Temple
tagline: Sri Sri Kalika Maharani Temple — spiritual town of Jharkhand

heroImage.alt: Sri Sri Kalika Maharani Temple, Jharkhand

overview.short: Sri Sri Kalika Maharani Temple is a Hindu temple in Bokaro in the state of Jharkhand, India is a Hindu temple at Chira Chas, built by Aashiyaan Estate Developers.
overview.description: Sri Sri Kalika Maharani Temple is a Hindu temple in Bokaro in the state of Jharkhand, India is a Hindu temple at Chira Chas, built by Aashiyaan Estate Developers. The temple is spread over half an acre and is dedicated to Goddess Kali. The mini township of Kalika Vihar is named after this temple.

topPlaces.name: Chira Chas
topPlaces.description: Chira Chas, part of Chas municipality (Chas Ward #2), in the state of Jharkhand, India is a suburb of Bokaro Steel City and Chas.Chira Chas is one of the fastest-growing urban regions in Jharkhand It is included in the urban area of Bokaro Steel City. Chira Chas is notable for its real estate boom, with many housing projects and Many colonies (Anand Vihar, Kunj Vihar, Chanakya Puri Colony, Great Alexander Colony, Gulshan Enclave and Indrapuri Lane) and integrated townships being built within its scenic environs.

topPlaces.name: Adarsh Vidya Mandir, Bokaro
topPlaces.description: Adarsh Vidya Mandir is a private higher senior Secondary school situated in Bokaro, Jharkhand, India. The school management is run by Marwari Panchayat, Chas Bokaro.

topPlaces.name: Chas, Bokaro
topPlaces.description: Chas (literally চাষ - खेती - Cultivation) is a Municipal Corporation in the Chas subdivision of the Bokaro district in the state of Jharkhand, India. It is often referred to as a suburb of Bokaro Steel City, though it predates the steel plant.

topPlaces.name: Chas block
topPlaces.description: Chas is a community development block that forms an administrative division in the Chas subdivision of the Bokaro district, Jharkhand state, India.

topPlaces.name: Bokaro Steel City
topPlaces.description: Bokaro, officially known as Bokaro Steel City (), is an industrial planned city in Jharkhand, India. With an estimated metropolitan population of 1.1 million and 183 square kilometres (71 sq mi) of area, it is the fourth largest city in the state.

topPlaces.name: City Centre, Bokaro
topPlaces.description: The City Centre is a prominent marketplace in Sector 4, Bokaro Steel City, Jharkhand, India. It is a CBD (Commercial Business District), and one of the most developed and planned CBDs in Jharkhand and Eastern India.

topPlaces.name: Bandhgora
topPlaces.description: Bandhgora is a census town in the Chas CD block in the Chas subdivision of the Bokaro district in the state of Jharkhand, India.

topPlaces.name: Pupunki
topPlaces.description: Pupunki is a village in Jharkhand state, India, located on the banks of the Damodar River, which separates the Dhanbad and Bokaro districts. National Highway 32 passes through the village, connecting it directly to Dhanbad and Bokaro, making the village easily accessible by road.

items.note: Chira Chas, part of Chas municipality (Chas Ward #2), in the state of Jharkhand, India is a suburb of Bokaro Steel City and Chas.Chira Chas is one of the fastest-growing urban regions in Jharkhand It is included in the urban area of Bokaro Steel City. Chira Chas is notable for its real estate boom, with many housing projects and Many colonies (Anand Vihar, Kunj Vihar, Chanakya Puri Colony, Great Alexander Colony, Gulshan Enclave and Indrapuri Lane) and integrated townships being built within its scenic environs.
Adarsh Vidya Mandir is a private higher senior Secondary school situated in Bokaro, Jharkhand, India. The school management is run by Marwari Panchayat, Chas Bokaro.

Chas (literally চাষ - खेती - Cultivation) is a Municipal Corporation in the Chas subdivision of the Bokaro district in the state of Jharkhand, India. It is often referred to as a suburb of Bokaro Steel City, though it predates the steel plant.
Chas is a community development block that forms an administrative division in the Chas subdivision of the Bokaro district, Jharkhand state, India.

Bokaro, officially known as Bokaro Steel City (), is an industrial planned city in Jharkhand, India. With an estimated metropolitan population of 1.1 million and 183 square kilometres (71 sq mi) of area, it is the fourth largest city in the state.

The City Centre is a prominent marketplace in Sector 4, Bokaro Steel City, Jharkhand, India. It is a CBD (Commercial Business District), and one of the most developed and planned CBDs in Jharkhand and Eastern India.
Bandhgora is a census town in the Chas CD block in the Chas subdivision of the Bokaro district in the state of Jharkhand, India.

Pupunki is a village in Jharkhand state, India, located on the banks of the Damodar River, which separates the Dhanbad and Bokaro districts. National Highway 32 passes through the village, connecting it directly to Dhanbad and Bokaro, making the village easily accessible by road.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Sri Sri Kalika Maharani Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Sri Sri Kalika Maharani Temple is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Sri Sri Kalika Maharani Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Birsa Munda Airport, Ranchi (94 km) and the nearest railway station is Dhanbad Junction (31 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Sri Sri Kalika Maharani Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Sri Sri Kalika Maharani Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Sri Sri Kalika Maharani Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Sri Sri Kalika Maharani Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Sri Sri Kalika Maharani Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Chira Chas, Adarsh Vidya Mandir, Bokaro, Chas, Bokaro, Chas block, Bokaro Steel City.
